---
title: "Data Based Analysis"
---

## Overview

This chapter explores emerging frontiers in urban morphology: urban morphometrics, spatial morphology, and spatial data science. Urban morphometrics is a quantitative field aiming at extracting patterns from large amounts of morphological data; spatial morphology expands the field to the analysis of unbuilt space mostly using space syntax theory and landscape ecology principles; spatial data science as a cross-cutting field increasingly adopts urban morphological information to enrich the understanding of spatial phenomena of environmental, social and economic kind.


## Techniques


```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(igraph)
library(visNetwork)
library(dplyr)
library(htmlwidgets)

# 1. Load your data
nodes <- data.frame(
  id = 1:3,
  label = c("Node A", "Node B", "Node C"),
  title = c("Click to go to Chapter 1", "Click for Chapter 2", "Click for Chapter 3"),
  url = c("../catalog/examples/bindels_gietema.html", "../catalog/examples/ijburg.html",
          "../catalog/examples/kumbh_mela.html")
)

# Force everything to be a simple, non-factor data frame
nodes_clean <- as.data.frame(nodes) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) %>% # Convert to strings for safety
  mutate(id = as.numeric(id))                   # But ID must stay a number

edges <- data.frame(
  from = c(1, 2),
  to = c(3, 3),
  value = c(10, 2) # Relation strength
)

edges <- edges %>%
  mutate(
    # Use a power function to make 'strong' relations significantly shorter
    length = 1000 / (value^2) 
  )

visNetwork(nodes_clean, edges) %>%
  visPhysics(
    solver = "forceAtlas2Based",
    forceAtlas2Based = list(
      springLength = 0,        # Force it to use our 'length' column
      springConstant = 0.1,    # Higher = 'stiffer' springs
      gravitationalConstant = -50 # Lower = nodes push each other away less
    )
  ) %>%



# 2. Create the interactive graph
# visNetwork(nodes, edges) %>%
#   visNodes(shape = "dot") %>%
#   visEdges(smooth = FALSE) %>%
#   visPhysics(solver = "forceAtlas2Based", 
#              forceAtlas2Based = list(springLength = 0)) %>%
  # Use selectNode and wrap in JS()
  visEvents(selectNode = htmlwidgets::JS("
    function(params) {
      // 1. Get the raw data for the clicked node
      var nodeData = this.body.data.nodes.get(params.nodes);
      
      // 2. The 'Deep Dig': If it's an array, grab the first element (repeatedly if needed)
      while (Array.isArray(nodeData) && nodeData.length > 0) {
        nodeData = nodeData;
      }

      console.log('Target Node Data:', nodeData);

      // 3. Navigate if the URL exists
      if (nodeData && nodeData.url) {
        console.log('Success! Moving to:', nodeData.url);
        window.location.href = nodeData.url;
      } else {
        console.error('Still no URL! Available properties:', Object.keys(nodeData));
      }
    }
  "))





```
